queue.description: Build the 6 axes {dollar, tick, vol, range, logdollar, entropy} in bar_builder.py; add NEW LogDollarBarBuilder (cumulative log(1+close*vol)) and EntropyBarBuilder (TRAIN-frozen 5-bucket Shannon-surprise accumulator, causal). Measure TRAIN bar-return excess kurtosis per axis.
queue.hypothesis: H1: the four information-driven axes (vol, dollar, logdollar, entropy) have lower TRAIN bar-return excess kurtosis than count/price axes (tick, range) AND higher median REAL OOS Calmar. Reject if info axes do not have lower median kurtosis OR their median REAL Calmar is <= that of {tick, range}.

queue.technique: Kurtosis is the mechanism, not a correlate
queue.description: Across all 48 cells per ticker, rank-correlate (-TRAIN bar-return kurtosis) against REAL OOS Calmar, pooled and within-ticker.
queue.hypothesis: H1b: rank correlation rho between -kurtosis and Calmar > 0 (pooled and within ticker). Reject if rho <= 0 or not directionally consistent across >=2 tickers. Guards against 'more bars always wins regardless of distribution'.

queue.description: Existing labeler. Stage-1 KMeans(2) on forward-vol -> low-vol cluster; Stage-2 KMeans({2,3}) on standardized [fwd_ret, |fwd_ret|] within low-vol; up-cluster->1, else 0, high-vol->-1 (abstain). Sweep horizons {50,100,200} and nc {2,3}; pick VAL balance in (0.2,0.8). Centers/scaler TRAIN-frozen, VAL/TEST predict-assigned.
queue.hypothesis: H2: featured clustering labelers beat max(hmm, always_long) baseline on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers.

queue.description: Existing labeler. TRAIN median forward-vol med_v; fwd_vol<=med_v ->1, else 0. Sweep horizons; VAL balance gate (0.2,0.8). med_v is a TRAIN scalar applied forward.
queue.hypothesis: H2: carry (featured) beats max(hmm, always_long) on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers; expected strongest on gold/metals (GLD pilot).

queue.description: Existing labeler. TRAIN 33rd/67th percentiles of forward return; fwd_ret>=top ->1, <=bottom ->0, middle third ->-1 (abstain). TRAIN balance gate (0.3,0.7). Percentile cuts are TRAIN scalars.
queue.hypothesis: H2: tertile (featured) beats max(hmm, always_long) on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers.

queue.description: Existing labeler. KMeans-2 vol filter, then BayesianGaussianMixture(n_components {3,4,5}, covariance_type='full', weight_concentration_prior=0.1, n_init=3, max_iter=300, random_state=42) on low-vol [fwd_ret,|fwd_ret|]. up-component=max mean fwd_ret; label 1 iff predict==up AND posterior(up)>0.40, else 0; high-vol->-1. Sparse Dirichlet self-prunes K. GMM+scaler TRAIN-fit; predict_proba forward.
queue.hypothesis: H2: bgm (featured) beats max(hmm, always_long) on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers.

queue.technique: agglomerative labeler (Ward) with TRAIN-centroid 1-NN forward assignment
queue.description: Existing labeler, made OOS-applicable. Vol filter, then AgglomerativeClustering(n_clusters {2,3,4}, linkage='ward') on TRAIN low-vol [fwd_ret,|fwd_ret|]. up-cluster=max mean fwd_ret; up->1 else 0; high-vol->-1. Agglomerative has no native predict: compute centroids from TRAIN cluster membership, assign VAL/TEST by nearest TRAIN centroid (1-NN). Do NOT refit jointly.
queue.hypothesis: H2: agglomerative (featured) beats max(hmm, always_long) on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers; historically GLD-specific.

queue.description: Featured AFML labeler: profit-take / stop-loss / time-out barriers on forward path; long when upper barrier hit first. TRAIN-fit barrier widths (sigma-scaled) frozen and replayed forward. Forward look-ahead is inside the label only (Wang: labels may peek forward); features and model stay causal (G3 audits feature/model leaks, not the label window).
queue.hypothesis: H2: triple_barrier (featured) beats max(hmm, always_long) on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers.

queue.description: NEW. Run base labeler (default kmeans2stage) independently at h in {50,100,200}. Trade only where all three agree: 1 iff all h==1; 0 iff all h==0; else -1 (abstain). Wang multi-scale consensus at the LABEL level (distinct from trainer seed-consensus). Each per-horizon labeler TRAIN-fit and frozen.
queue.hypothesis: H2: multi_horizon (featured) beats max(hmm, always_long) on REAL OOS Calmar, replicated across >=2 tickers.

queue.technique: Axis x labeler interaction is real (not separable)
queue.description: From the 6x8 factorial Calmar matrix per ticker, test whether the best labeler depends on the axis (non-trivial interaction), e.g. ensemble-style labelers unlock vol bars but hurt dollar bars.
queue.hypothesis: H3: the best labeler differs by axis. Reject if the same labeler is optimal on every axis for every ticker (pure main effects, no interaction).

queue.technique: Structural fat tails on HYG/XLE survive any axis
queue.description: Reserved for full study (not pilot). On HYG (kurt >=28 every axis) and XLE (kurt >=12), check no axis brings TRAIN kurtosis near-Gaussian and Calmar stays below panel median.
queue.hypothesis: H5: no axis drives HYG or XLE TRAIN kurt below ~5 while lifting Calmar to panel-median. Reject if some axis does both.

queue.description: BASELINE. Wang does NOT use HMM as a method (he dislikes HMM-as-trading-model; HMM is global-optimum only, cannot do online causal inference). GaussianHMM(n_components=3, covariance_type='diag', n_iter=100, random_state=42) on TRAIN bar obs [r_bar, |r_bar|]. Sort states by mean of dim 0; up=highest. Use CAUSAL forward-filter posterior phi_t^up (NOT smoothed/Viterbi -- smoothing peeks at future bars). y=1{phi>0.5} else 0; never abstains (dense baseline). Params TRAIN-fit; forward filter applied causally at VAL/TEST.
queue.hypothesis: Baseline: featured labelers are expected to BEAT this. It is the strongest HMM config (Q2 3-state [r,|r|]) so the featured-vs-baseline test is conservative.

queue.description: BASELINE. y=1 for every labeled bar; no direction info at label stage. Downstream model still trains on features. REAL Calmar ~ buy-and-hold-while-above-threshold floor. Quantifies how much of a cell's Calmar is just drift capture vs genuine regime signal. Hard to beat on drift-heavy ETFs (QQQ beta200=0.74).
queue.hypothesis: Baseline: featured labelers must beat max(hmm, always_long). Featured-vs-baseline lift reported per ticker as Calmar(best featured) - Calmar(best baseline).
